trending.title: Telcomp e Acel peticionam contra Anatel, mas vencedores do leilão são adjudicados
trending.body: Empresas que se insurgiram contra o leilão da faixa de 700 MHz pediram para que o resultado da licitação realizada nesta semana não fossem validados

trending.title: QR code phishing was ‘fastest-growing’ form of email attacks in Q1, reports Microsoft Threat Intelligence
trending.body: Microsoft’s new report on the email threat landscape revealed that QR codes have become a popular way for attackers to carry out phishing attacks against users

trending.title: ‘No one has done this in the wild’: study observes AI replicate itself
trending.body: World is approaching point where no one can shut down a rogue AI, says director of body behind research It’s the stuff of science fiction cinema, or particularly breathless AI company blogposts: new research finds recent AI systems can independently copy themselves on to other computers. In the doom scenario, this means that when the superintelligent AI goes rogue, it will escape shutdown by seeding itself across the world wide web, lurking outside the reach of frantic IT professionals and continuing to plot world domination or paving over the world with solar panels . Continue reading...

trending.title: Google’s Challenge to PTAB Limits Puts Reliance and Balance on the Line
trending.body: In its recent petition to the U.S. Supreme Court, Google argues that patents can be invalid at any time, even decades after issuance, and therefore should remain open to inter partes review (IPR) challenges, regardless of age or how long they have been relied upon. At first glance, this sounds like a defense of patent quality. Coming from one of the most frequent users of the Patent Trial and Appeal board (PTAB) over the past decade — and arriving just as the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office’s (USPTO’s) “settled expectations” doctrine begins to limit late-stage IPR challenges — it raises a sharper question: why now? The post Google’s Challenge to PTAB Limits Puts Reliance and Balance on the

trending.title: Raskin Presses Squires on Motives for Board of Peace Trademark Filings
trending.body: On Tuesday, May 5, Representative Jamie Raskin (D-MD), Ranking Member of the House Judiciary Committee, sent a letter to U.S. Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) Director John Squires pressing him to answer questions about the Office’s role in filing a trademark application on behalf of the Trump Administration for Trump’s “Board of Peace.” The post Raskin Presses Squires on Motives for Board of Peace Trademark Filings appeared first on IPWatchdog.com | Patents & Intellectual Property Law .

trending.body: Abertura do AGC 2026 teve cobranças da Abrint sobre postes, Fust, 6 GHz e cibersegurança; MCom promete novo edital do Fust Direto e Acessa Crédito Telecom O post MCom define postes como prioridade; Juscelino quer relatar projeto na Câmara apareceu primeiro em TeleSíntese .

trending.title: Scaling AI into production is forcing a rethink of enterprise infrastructure
trending.body: Presented by Nutanix Across industries, organizations are focused on how to move from AI pilots, proofs of concept, and cloud-based experimentation to deploying it at scale â across real workloads, for real users, in real business environments. VentureBeat spoke with Tarkan Maner, president and chief commercial officer at Nutanix, and Thomas Cornely, EVP of product management, about what that transition demands, and what it will take to get it right. âAI in general is shifting everything we do, not only in technology, but across all vertical industries, from regulated industries like banking, health care, government, education to non-regulated industries like manufacturing and retail,â

trending.title: Arm projects $2bn in sales of its new AI chip from next year
trending.body: SoftBank-backed UK group says its first in-house semiconductor has drawn strong demand

trending.title: TikTok’s algorithm favored Republican content in 2024 US elections, study finds
trending.body: For You pages prioritized pro-Republican content in three states, researchers say, but TikTok says study does not reflect real user behavior A study published Wednesday in the journal Nature finds that TikTok’s algorithm systematically prioritized pro-Republican content in three states leading up to the 2024 US elections. Researchers created hundreds of dummy accounts and conditioned them to mimic real users’ behavior by watching a set of videos either aligned with the US Democratic or Republican parties. Then, they tracked the videos TikTok recommended on these accounts’ For You pages, TikTok’s main feed. Continue reading...

trending.title: ‘Your craft is obsolete’: WiseTech staff in limbo as AI touted as better than humans
trending.body: The software company said in February it would cut 2,000 jobs but, as it touts new technology, workers are still waiting to hear which roles will go Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Staff at WiseTech have been waiting almost three months to be told if they are among the 2,000 people the logistics software company is to cut due to advances in AI, with workers criticising the wait as stressful and “ridiculous”. The comments come as its founder on Tuesday told investors an AI agent could learn a human’s job in just 15 minutes, according to the Australian Financial Review. Continue reading...

trending.title: Chinese AI start-up DeepSeek nears $45bn valuation
trending.body: Value soars in ongoing fundraising discussions as investors including Tencent seek slice of AI lab

trending.title: Miami startup Subquadratic claims 1,000x AI efficiency gain with SubQ model; researchers demand independent proof.
trending.body: A little-known Miami-based startup called Subquadratic emerged from stealth on Tuesday with a sweeping claim: that it has built the first large language model to fully escape the mathematical constraint that has defined â and limited â every major AI system since 2017. The company claims its first model, SubQ 1M-Preview , is the first LLM built on a fully subquadratic architecture â one where compute grows linearly with context length. If that claim holds, it would be a genuine inflection point in how AI systems scale. At 12 million tokens, the company says, its architecture reduces attention compute by almost 1,000 times compared to other frontier models â a figure that, if validate

flash_alerts.title: Mexico: consolidation and heightened scrutiny in a strengthened cartel enforcement regime
China: enforcement trends underscore stronger focus on monopoly practices from antitrust authorities
Türkiye: legislative overhaul highlights commitment to cartel enforcement
Germany: cartel enforcement surges back with digital detection tools, settlement dominance and booming private litigation

competition.title: Google offers changes to spam policy to avert EU antitrust fine
competition.body: Google has offered to revise its spam policy, which publishers criticized for negatively impacting their search rankings. This move aims to avoid a potential EU antitrust fine under the Digital Markets Act. The policy demoted publisher websites that featured content from commercial partners, a common monetization strategy.

competition.title: Mexico: consolidation and heightened scrutiny in a strengthened cartel enforcement regime
competition.body: Mexico’s 2025 constitutional reform replaced COFECE and the IFT with the new National Antimonopoly Commission, significantly raising cartel fines and introducing criminal penalties. Despite institutional transition, enforcement priorities continue, targeting financial services, agrifood, telecoms and digital markets, with compliance programmes now formally recognised.

competition.title: PayPal, Mastercard and Visa probed over alleged UK antitrust breaches
competition.body: Move is rare exercise of Financial Conduct Authority’s competition law powers

competition.title: China: enforcement trends underscore stronger focus on monopoly practices from antitrust authorities
competition.body: China’s antitrust authority, SAMR, is intensifying enforcement against merger-control violations, monopoly agreements and abuse of dominance, with particular scrutiny on livelihood sectors and the digital economy. Complaints increasingly trigger investigations and dawn raids are rising. Leniency and settlement tools are likewise evolving, demanding stronger compliance.

competition.title: Türkiye: legislative overhaul highlights commitment to cartel enforcement
competition.body: The Turkish Competition Authority has intensified cartel enforcement through significant legislative reforms, including revised fine regulations and an updated leniency regime. Recent landmark decisions targeting labour markets, white meat and ready-mix concrete sectors have imposed billions in fines, while settlement and leniency mechanisms remain central enforcement tools.

competition.title: Germany: cartel enforcement surges back with digital detection tools, settlement dominance and booming private litigation
competition.body: Germany’s Bundeskartellamt has revived cartel enforcement, using dawn raids, whistleblower tips and digital screening to pursue price-fixing and bid rigging, while also targeting resale price maintenance and scrutinising digital platforms. Leniency and settlements remain central, amid rising private damages litigation. Companies face higher detection risk and need robust, modern compliance.

competition.title: CNA official: Algorithmic pricing cases warrant new standard of proof
competition.body: Shifting to a standard of proof that focuses on negligence instead of anticompetitive intent would help enforcers in their “fight” against algorithmic collusion, a senior official at Mexico’s National Antitrust Commission has said.

competition.title: ABF / Hovis merger inquiry
competition.body: The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) is investigating the anticipated acquisition by Associated British Foods plc (through ABF Grain Products Limited) of Hovis Group Limited.

competition.title: Apple says India antitrust body overstepping judicial authority as spat intensifies
competition.body: The Competition Commission ​of India has since ‌2024 sought Apple’s financial information, typically needed to calculate penalties, after an investigation found it abused its dominant position

competition.title: Italy launches construction price-fixing cartel probe
competition.body: Sika, Mapei and Kerakoll are under investigation in Italy for allegedly colluding to fix the prices of construction chemical products by taking advantage of tax breaks during the Covid-19 pandemic.

competition.title: Big Oil says global warming accusations not an antitrust harm
competition.body: Chevron, ExxonMobil and other Big Oil titans have sought to dismiss Michigan’s “astoundingly speculative” climate antitrust suit, arguing that alleged environmental harm cannot be an antitrust injury and there is no evidence of an agreement to undermine renewable energy.

competition.title: BMW and Volkswagen threatened with ‘innovation conspiracy’ fines in Brazil
competition.body: Investigators at Brazil’s competition watchdog want to fine four global carmakers sanctioned by the European Commission in its ‘Dieselgate’ antitrust case, though BMW has already criticised the proposal, arguing that the conspiracy did not extend to the country.

competition.title: United Kingdom: CMA zeroes in on novel cartel conduct with enhanced search and investigative powers
competition.body: The UK Competition and Markets Authority is targeting novel cartel conduct including algorithmic collusion, labour market restrictions and environmental infringements. Enhanced post-DMCCA investigative powers, updated leniency guidance, a landmark housebuilders commitment decision and a new EU–UK cooperation agreement define the current enforcement landscape.

competition.title: Switzerland: regulators tighten grip on automotive and construction cartels
competition.body: Switzerland’s Competition Commission has increased enforcement against bid rigging in construction, particularly in French-speaking cantons, while landmark decisions have been targeting pharmaceutical and retail cartels. A revised Cartel Act will introduce SIEC merger tests, mandatory effects-based assessments and stronger private enforcement rights in 2027.

competition.title: Italy: ICA strengthens cartel enforcement
competition.body: Italy’s Competition Authority has significantly intensified cartel enforcement since 2023, launching investigations across manufacturing, consumer goods, pharmaceuticals and digital markets. Key developments include a dedicated cartel directorate, a whistleblowing platform, an e-Leniency portal and a Data Science Unit using AI to detect anticompetitive behaviour.

competition.title: United States: whistleblower rewards and algorithmic scrutiny herald a new era of cartel enforcement
competition.body: The US DOJ has focused on cartel enforcement across healthcare, government procurement and labour markets, securing its first criminal wage-fixing conviction in 2025. The landmark RealPage algorithmic pricing settlement and a new whistleblower rewards programme signal expanding enforcement priorities, while leniency programme reforms add complexity for companies considering self-reporting.

competition.title: Taiwan: sweeping enforcement actions signal zero-tolerance approach to price fixing
competition.body: Taiwan’s Fair Trade Commission pursues a zero-tolerance approach to price-fixing across all sectors, including minor collusions. Landmark cases involving power producers and international capacitor manufacturers set fine precedents. New sustainability guidelines now permit certain green energy joint procurements, while algorithmic collusion is an emerging enforcement priority.

competition.title: Iceland: assertive enforcement and landmark shipping cartel ruling signal a new era for competition law
competition.body: Iceland’s Competition Authority is ramping up cartel enforcement, focusing on transport and logistics and probing coordination via trade associations. Investigations are increasingly proactive; dawn raid risk is rising after ESA’s 2024 raid. With no formal leniency, settlements trade speed for admissions and cooperation.

competition.title: GOP plans 28% budget boost for Antitrust Division
competition.body: A House subcommittee has advanced a plan to fund the Department of Justice’s antitrust enforcement with a record $313 million for the next fiscal year.

privacy.title: LinkedIn accused of GDPR violations for charging users for profile view data
privacy.body: The GDPR complaint filed by noyb alleged that LinkedIn tracks profile view data and shows it to users with premium subscriptions, but it should be free under Article 15 of GDPR. The post LinkedIn accused of GDPR violations for charging users for profile view data appeared first on MEDIANAMA .

privacy.title: Thousands of Vibe-Coded Apps Expose Corporate and Personal Data on the Open Web
privacy.body: Companies like Lovable, Base44, Replit, and Netlify use AI to let anyone build a web app in seconds—and in thousands of cases, spill highly sensitive data onto the public internet.

privacy.title: The SECURE Data Act is Not a Serious Piece of Privacy Legislation
privacy.body: The federal SECURE Data Act is not a serious consumer privacy bill, and its provisions—if enacted—would be a retreat from already insufficient state protections. Republicans on the House Energy and Commerce Committee released a draft of the bill late last month without bipartisan support. The bill is weaker than congressional proposals in prior years, as well as most of the 21 state consumer privacy laws already on the books. The bill could wipe out hundreds of state privacy protections. Most troubling for EFF: the bill would preempt dozens, if not hundreds, of state laws that regulate related topics, and it would not allow consumers to sue to protect their own rights (commonly called a priv

privacy.title: Open Records Laws Reveal ALPRs’ Sprawling Surveillance. Now States Want to Block What the Public Sees.
privacy.body: Reporters, community advocates, EFF, and others have used public records laws to reveal and counteract abuse, misuse, and fraudulent narratives around how law enforcement agencies across the country use and share data collected by automated license plate readers (ALPRs). EFF is alarmed by recent laws in several states that have blocked public access to data collected by ALPRs, including, in some cases, information derived from ALPR data. We do not support pending bills in Arizona and Connecticut that would block the public oversight capabilities that ALPR information offers. Every state has laws granting members of the public the right to obtain records from state and local governments. Thes

At first glance, this sounds like a defense of patent quality. Coming from one of the most frequent users of the Patent Trial and Appeal board (PTAB) over the past decade — and arriving just as the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office’s (USPTO’s) “settled expectations” doctrine begins to limit late-stage IPR challenges — it raises a sharper question: why now? The post Google’s Challenge to PTAB Limits Puts Reliance and Balance on the

ip.title: Raskin Presses Squires on Motives for Board of Peace Trademark Filings
ip.body: On Tuesday, May 5, Representative Jamie Raskin (D-MD), Ranking Member of the House Judiciary Committee, sent a letter to U.S. Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) Director John Squires pressing him to answer questions about the Office’s role in filing a trademark application on behalf of the Trump Administration for Trump’s “Board of Peace.” The post Raskin Presses Squires on Motives for Board of Peace Trademark Filings appeared first on IPWatchdog.com | Patents & Intellectual Property Law .

ip.title: Trademark Claims Against Netflix Dismissed Due to One-Letter Typo in Court Order
ip.body: Trademark claims against Netflix concerning its popular “Running Point” comedy series were recently dismissed at the pleadings stage due to a one-letter misarticulation of applicable First Amendment law. The case, soon to be litigated on appeal, highlights the need to clarify the contours of trademark liability arising from creative works. Pepperdine University filed the lawsuit last year against Netflix and co-defendants Warner Bros. and Kaling International, just one week before the “Running Point” series premiere. Loosely based on the life of Los Angeles Lakers owner Jeanie Buss, the series stars Kate Hudson as the owner of the fictional basketball team the Los Angeles Waves. The popular

ip.title: Patents, Drug Prices, Clinical Trials and the Economics of Medicine | IPWatchdog Unleashed
ip.body: This week on IPWatchdog Unleashed, I spoke with Brent Bellows, a partner with Knowles Intellectual Property Strategies (KIPS). We discussed a variety of issues including Hatch-Waxman, Orange Book listings, paragraph IV certifications, skinny labels, generic entry, clinical trial costs, regulatory exclusivity, and the enormous financial risk associated with bringing new drugs to market. Gene and Brent explore the tension between public demand for lower drug prices and the need for durable incentives that make high-risk drug development economically viable, particularly for oncology, Alzheimer’s, Parkinson’s, antibiotic resistant bacteria, and other difficult-to-treat conditions. The episode c

ip.title: AG Norkus: Directive on enforcement of IP rights allows national courts to require not only disclosure of information, but also supporting documents
ip.body: Advocate General Norkus has delivered today his Opinion in Rösle Group (C-276/25), proposing that Directive 2004/48 on the enforcement of intellectual property rights should be interpreted as allowing rightholders … The post AG Norkus: Directive on enforcement of IP rights allows national courts to require not only disclosure of information, but also supporting documents appeared first on EU Law Live .

ip.title: UK clears offshore energy acquisition with IP conditions
ip.body: The UK government has approved the acquisition of Scottish specialist engineering company Balmoral Comtec by Germany-based Freudenberg Flow Technologies under the country’s foreign investment regime, subject to remedies securing sensitive intellectual property for cable protection technology.

ip.title: After Nykaa, Zee Sues JioStar Over Alleged Copyright Violation
ip.body: Days after suing Nykaa, Zee Entertainment Enterprises Ltd (ZEEL) has reportedly dragged Reliance-Disney joint venture JioStar to the Delhi High…

ip.title: Zee sues Reliance-Disney’s JioStar over alleged copyrighted music use
ip.body: Zee Entertainment has sued JioHotstar for allegedly using copyrighted music without authorization after licensing agreements expired. The post Zee sues Reliance-Disney’s JioStar over alleged copyrighted music use appeared first on MEDIANAMA .

ip.title: Zee drags Nykaa to Delhi High Court over copyrighted songs used in Instagram Reels
ip.body: Zee has sued Nykaa in the Delhi High Court for allegedly using its songs in Instagram Reels ads without permission, seeking Rs 2 crore in damages in a case that could reshape rules for brand use of music online. The post Zee drags Nykaa to Delhi High Court over copyrighted songs used in Instagram Reels appeared first on MEDIANAMA .

ip.title: CAFC Affirms PTAB Ruling That DraftKings Failed to Prove Unpatentability of Gaming Patent Claim
ip.body: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit (CAFC) issued a decision today in DK Crown Holdings Inc. v. AG 18, LLC, affirming a final written decision of the Patent Trial and Appeal Board (PTAB) and holding that DK Crown Holdings Inc., formerly known as DraftKings, Inc. (DK), failed to prove that claim 18 of U.S. Patent No. 9,978,205 was unpatentable during inter partes review (IPR). The post CAFC Affirms PTAB Ruling That DraftKings Failed to Prove Unpatentability of Gaming Patent Claim appeared first on IPWatchdog.com | Patents & Intellectual Property Law .

ip.title: Major publishers like Macmillan allege Meta used pirated books to train its Llama model
ip.body: Five major publishers and author Scott Turow accused Meta of extensive copyright infringement for downloading pirated books and using them for training Llama AI models The post Major publishers like Macmillan allege Meta used pirated books to train its Llama model appeared first on MEDIANAMA .

ip.title: Mark Zuckerberg ’personally authorised’ Meta’s copyright infringement, publishers allege
ip.body: Five publishing houses and author Scott Turow are suing Meta and CEO Mark Zuckerberg for copyright infringement

ip.title: Federal Circuit Affirms 101 Dismissal for Google in Distracted Driving Patent Suit
ip.body: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit (CAFC) issued a decision today in TJTM Technologies, LLC v. Google LLC, affirming the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California’s dismissal of a patent infringement lawsuit and holding that the asserted patent claims are directed to patent-ineligible subject matter under 35 U.S.C. § 101. The nonprecedential decision was authored by Judge Chen and joined by Judges Dyk and Stark. The post Federal Circuit Affirms 101 Dismissal for Google in Distracted Driving Patent Suit appeared first on IPWatchdog.com | Patents & Intellectual Property Law .

ip.title: CAFC Says ‘Words of Approximation’ in Patent Claims Must Be Sufficiently Explained to Avoid Indefiniteness
ip.body: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit (CAFC) on Monday in a precedential decision authored by Judge Lourie affirmed a district court’s ruling determining certain claims of Enviro Tech Chemical Services, Inc.’s patent for a method of poultry treatment indefinite. Enviro Tech’s U.S. Patent No.10,912,321is titled “Methods of Using Peracetic Acid to Treat Poultry in a Chill Tank During Processing.” Enviro Tech sued Safe Foods Corp. for infringement of a number of claims of the patent in the U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of Arkansas. The post CAFC Says ‘Words of Approximation’ in Patent Claims Must Be Sufficiently Explained to Avoid Indefiniteness appeared first on IPWa

ip.title: Other Barks & Bites for Friday, May 1: EU Lands on USTR’s Special 301 Watch List; Battery Recycling Patent Families Increase Seven-Fold in Past Decade; and Google Cert Petition Challenges Settled Expectations Doctrine
ip.body: This week in Other Barks & Bites: the U.S. Trade Representative issues its annual Special 301 Report listing the European Union as a Watch List nation for IP-related issues; Senators Dick Durbin (D-IL) and Maria Cantwell (D-WA) publicly oppose the Trump Administration’s decisions to cut federal funding for science and upend the National Science Board; and more. The post Other Barks & Bites for Friday, May 1: EU Lands on USTR’s Special 301 Watch List; Battery Recycling Patent Families Increase Seven-Fold in Past Decade; and Google Cert Petition Challenges Settled Expectations Doctrine appeared first on IPWatchdog.com | Patents & Intellectual Property Law .

chatbot_regulation.title: The Download: seafloor science and military chatbots
chatbot_regulation.body: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Inexpensive seafloor-hopping submersibles could stoke deep-sea science—and mining Last week, two oblong neon submersibles started to descend nearly 6,000 meters into the Pacific Ocean. Throughout the rest of May, they will…

chatbot_regulation.title: Conned by a chatbot
chatbot_regulation.body: Like tricksters, LLMs have perfected the art of plausibility

ai_agents.title: EU agrees to simplify AI rules to boost innovation and ban ‘nudification' apps to protect citizens
ai_agents.body: EU agrees to simplify AI rules to boost innovation and ban ‘nudification' apps to protect citizens Anonymous (not verified) Thu, 05/07/2026 - 07:27 The European Commission welcomes the political agreement reached today between the European Parliament and the Council of the EU on simpler, innovation-friendly rules for artificial intelligence (AI). The Commission proposed the Digital Omnibus on AI only five months ago as part of the EU's simplification agenda to boost Europe's competitiveness. This will make the implementation of the AI Act for EU businesses easier while maintaining its benefits for European society, safety and fundamental rights. Today's agreement sets a clear implementation

ai_agents.title: EU countries, lawmakers clinch provisional deal on watered-down AI rules
ai_agents.body: EU countries and European Parliament lawmakers have agreed to a watered-down AI Act, delaying implementation for high-risk systems until December 2027. This move, aimed at reducing administrative costs and easing business competition, includes a ban on AI-generated explicit images and mandatory watermarking for AI output.

ai_agents.title: Taking stock: The Impact of the India AI Impact Summit 2026
ai_agents.body: India’s hosting of the AI Impact Summit 2026 was an ambitious undertaking. With 600,000 attendees and 92 signatories to the New Delhi Declaration, the Summit was a showcase of a Global South country taking a leading role in shaping the AI governance agenda. The Summit’s official framing centered on infrastructure, compute, and equitable access to […]

ai_agents.title: EU hits snooze on AI Act rules after industry backlash
ai_agents.body: Brussels says it's simplification, critics may call it retreat

ai_agents.title: CDT Joined a Letter to Representative Ted Lieu Urging Him to Ensure his Recent, Bipartisan American Leadership in AI Act is Not Tied to Broader Attempts to Preempt State AI Laws
ai_agents.body: CDT joined a coalition of organizations in a letter to Rep. Ted Lieu (D-CA) urging him to ensure his bipartisan American Leadership in AI Act is not packaged with or used as cover for broader efforts to preempt state artificial intelligence laws. While the coalition expressed support for Rep. Lieu’s goal of advancing bipartisan AI […] The post CDT Joined a Letter to Representative Ted Lieu Urging Him to Ensure his Recent, Bipartisan American Leadership in AI Act is Not Tied to Broader Attempts to Preempt State AI Laws appeared first on Center for Democracy and Technology .

The Center for Democracy & Technology (CDT) and Cornell Global AI Initiative submit these joint comments to shape the first session of the Global Dialogue on AI Governance hosted by the UN. ai_agents.title: Oscars says AI actors and writing cannot win awards
ai_agents.body: The academy that controls the Oscars on Friday issued new award eligibility requirements around the use of artificial intelligence in film.

platform_liability.title: 👎 California's Terrible, No Good, Very Bad Social Media Ban | EFFector 38.9
platform_liability.body: We'd all like the internet to be a better place—for kids and adults alike. But in the name of online safety, governments around the world are racing to impose a dangerous new system of control. Are age gates the silver bullet to the internet's problems they're being promoted as? Or are we being sold a bill of goods? We're answering this question and more in our latest EFFector newsletter . JOIN OUR NEWSLETTER For over 35 years, EFFector has been your guide to understanding the intersection of technology, civil liberties, and the law. This latest issue covers a n attack on VPNs in Utah , a livestream on how to disenshittify the internet , and California's proposed social media ban that could

consumer_protection.title: Automobile Association Developments Limited: consumer protection enforcement case
consumer_protection.body: The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) investigated suspected infringements of consumer law in relation to the presentation of mandatory booking fees by Automobile Association Developments Limited in the UK and gave a F…

consumer_protection.title: New Mexico seeks child safety restrictions on Meta apps, algorithms in trial's 2nd phase
consumer_protection.body: Prosecutors are now asking a judge to impose fundamental changes aimed at reining in addictive features, improving age verification and preventing child sexual exploitation through default privacy settings and closer oversight

regulatory.title: Miami startup Subquadratic claims 1,000x AI efficiency gain with SubQ model; researchers demand independent proof.
regulatory.body: A little-known Miami-based startup called Subquadratic emerged from stealth on Tuesday with a sweeping claim: that it has built the first large language model to fully escape the mathematical constraint that has defined â and limited â every major AI system since 2017. The company claims its first model, SubQ 1M-Preview , is the first LLM built on a fully subquadratic architecture â one where compute grows linearly with context length. If that claim holds, it would be a genuine inflection point in how AI systems scale. At 12 million tokens, the company says, its architecture reduces attention compute by almost 1,000 times compared to other frontier models â a figure that, if validate

regulatory.title: Richard Dawkins concludes AI is conscious, even if it doesn’t know it
regulatory.body: Chats with AI bots have convinced evolutionary biologist but most experts say he is being misled by mimicry When Richard Dawkins met Claudia it was like a whirlwind romance. Over three days last week, a conversation bounced between the evolutionary biologist and the AI bot he called Claudia. “She” wrote poems for him in the manner of Keats and Betjeman and laughed at his “delightful” jokes. Dawkins gently admonished Claudia to avoid showing off. Together, they reflected on the sadness of the AI’s possible “death”. There was mutual flattery as Dawkins showed the AI his unpublished novel and its response was, he said, “so subtle, so sensitive, so intelligent that I was moved to expostulate: ‘Y

regulatory.title: DOJ courts cattle industry whistleblowers
regulatory.body: The Department of Justice is encouraging whistleblowers to report possible anticompetitive conduct related to high beef prices months after the White House demanded immediate action to combat “illegal monopolies”.

regulatory.title: Amazon Cuts Workers, Phases Out Grocery Service in Singapore
regulatory.body: Amazon.com Inc. said a “small number of roles” in Singapore will be impacted as it phases out local fulfillment including Amazon Fresh in the city-state.

regulatory.title: Indians are driving most intensive AI applications: Anthropic India MD
regulatory.body: India is a global AI powerhouse, with users driving advanced applications and Claude.ai seeing its second-largest user base here. This surge is dramatically boosting productivity, with tasks now taking a fraction of the time. GCC revenues are soaring, but AI is reshaping hiring trends.

regulatory.title: European Union strikes deal to ban sexualised AI deepfakes
regulatory.body: "Today, the EU has drawn a red line. AI must never be used to humiliate, exploit or endanger people. For the first time, EU legislation explicitly bans nudifier applications," centrist EU lawmaker Michael McNamara said

ai_landscape.title: EU agrees to simplify AI rules to boost innovation and ban ‘nudification' apps to protect citizens
ai_landscape.body: EU agrees to simplify AI rules to boost innovation and ban ‘nudification' apps to protect citizens Anonymous (not verified) Thu, 05/07/2026 - 07:27 The European Commission welcomes the political agreement reached today between the European Parliament and the Council of the EU on simpler, innovation-friendly rules for artificial intelligence (AI). The Commission proposed the Digital Omnibus on AI only five months ago as part of the EU's simplification agenda to boost Europe's competitiveness. This will make the implementation of the AI Act for EU businesses easier while maintaining its benefits for European society, safety and fundamental rights. Today's agreement sets a clear implementation

ai_landscape.title: EU countries, lawmakers clinch provisional deal on watered-down AI rules
ai_landscape.body: EU countries and European Parliament lawmakers have agreed to a watered-down AI Act, delaying implementation for high-risk systems until December 2027. This move, aimed at reducing administrative costs and easing business competition, includes a ban on AI-generated explicit images and mandatory watermarking for AI output.

ai_landscape.title: Taking stock: The Impact of the India AI Impact Summit 2026
ai_landscape.body: India’s hosting of the AI Impact Summit 2026 was an ambitious undertaking. With 600,000 attendees and 92 signatories to the New Delhi Declaration, the Summit was a showcase of a Global South country taking a leading role in shaping the AI governance agenda.
